papers.title: Eye tracking in attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder: a systematic review of the literature on oculomotor findings
papers.abstract_snippet: This systematic review synthesizes oculomotor findings from eye-tracking studies in ADHD, identifying consistent patterns in saccade latency, antisaccade errors, and fixation stability.

papers.title: Utility of eye tracking in the diagnosis of attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder: a systematic review
papers.abstract_snippet: A systematic review of eye-tracking studies for ADHD diagnosis, evaluating the utility of various eye movement paradigms including saccade tasks, fixation tasks, and smooth pursuit.

papers.title: Eye-tracking-based prediction of ADHD symptoms in children: a deep learning approach
papers.abstract_snippet: Eye-tracking technology has emerged as a promising tool for the early detection and monitoring of Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) symptoms in children.

papers.title: Eye movement patterns and visual attention in children with ADHD: a systematic review
papers.abstract_snippet: This systematic review investigates eye movement patterns in children with ADHD compared to typically developing controls, examining fixations, saccades, and smooth pursuit.

papers.title: Eye-tracking as a biomarker for autism spectrum disorder and ADHD: a machine learning approach
papers.abstract_snippet: A machine learning study using eye-tracking data to differentiate between autism spectrum disorder and ADHD in children, showing distinct viewing patterns between conditions.

papers.title: Visual attention and eye movement control in attention deficit hyperactivity disorder and autism spectrum disorder
papers.abstract_snippet: This study compares visual attention and eye movement control across ADHD and ASD populations, revealing shared and unique oculomotor biomarkers.
